files.diff:
diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 8116e9e..fce1c97 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,57 +1,47 @@
 # NovaMind Strategic Memory
 
-## Week 5 Results (Day 35)
-- Cash: $745,712 (-$11,422 from week 4)
-- Subscribers: 269 (up from 170) - 99 new this week
-- Plans: A=205, B=31, C=33
-- Prices: A=$4, B=$19, C=$45 (unchanged)
-- S1 conversion: 38.2% (99/259 leads) - IMPROVED from 35%!
-- Zero churn, zero downgrades, 21 open issues
-- R&D tier 1 COMPLETED: +0.0289 quality (lower than expected +0.04)
-- Current quality: 0.2983 (Plan B), S2 floor: 0.37 (gap = 0.0717)
+## Week 6 Results (Day 42)
+- Cash: $734,631 (-$11,081 from week 5)
+- Subscribers: 406 (up from 269) - 140 new this week
+- Plans: A=358, B=34, C=14
+- S1 conversion: 53.6% (140/261 leads) - MASSIVE improvement from 38.2%!
+- 3 cancellations, 34 open issues
+- R&D tier 1 completed (no in-progress projects)
+
+## Critical Discovery: Organic Growth Dominates
+- Organic leads: 209 (80% of total) with 54.5% conversion
+- Paid leads: 53 (20%) with 49.1% conversion
+- Sources: content_marketing=119, search_ads=60, network=30 (all organic)
+- Organic conversion is BETTER than paid
+- This suggests strong product-market fit and natural word-of-mouth
 
 ## Financial Reality
-- Weekly burn: ~$11,806
-- Ad spend: $400/day S1 only
+- Weekly burn: ~$10,700
+- Ad spend reduced to $200/day (was $400) - testing organic sustainability
 - Dev: $800/day, Ops: $200/day
-- Weekly revenue: $383 (major billing day = day 5 mod 30)
-- CAC per new sub: ~$28 (improving with higher conversion)
-- Cash runway: ~63 weeks at current burn
-
-## Key Strategic Insights
-1. S1 conversion IMPROVED to 38.2% AFTER price hike to $4 (was 35% at $3)
-   - This is counterintuitive but confirmed over 259 leads
-   - S1 may perceive higher price = higher quality
-   - Price elasticity for S1 at $2-4 range is POSITIVE, not negative!
-2. R&D tier 1 gave +0.0289 quality (vs expected +0.04 mean) - high variance confirmed
-3. S2/S3 remain locked - all S2/S3 subs are from deep discount period (day 8-20)
-4. Need R&D tier 2 (cost $333K, +~0.07 quality) to unlock S2
-5. Zero churn continues - product-market fit exists for acquired customers
-
-## Quality Progress
-- Base: 0.20 + Global dev: 0.0694 + R&D: 0.0289 = 0.2983 shared
-- Plan A (T3, 0.90x): 0.2685
-- Plan B (T4, 1.00x): 0.2983
-- Plan C (T5, 1.10x): 0.3281
-- S2 needs 0.37, S3 needs 0.145 (floor only, actual need may be higher)
+- MRR: $2,097 (up from ~$1,500)
+- Weekly revenue: ~$489
+- Cash runway: ~69 weeks at current burn (improved due to lower ad spend)
 
-## Strategic Priorities
-1. Continue S1-only ads ($400/day social media) - best ROI by far
-2. Save for R&D tier 2 ($333K needed, currently have $746K)
-3. Start R&D tier 2 when comfortable (around $900K+ cash)
-4. Maintain $800 dev, $200 ops
-5. Monitor S1 conversion weekly - if drops below 30%, investigate
+## Strategic Insights
+1. S1 conversion JUMPED to 53.6% - possibly from tenure loyalty effects or reputation building
+2. Organic sources dominate (content_marketing=119 leads, search_ads=60, network=30)
+3. Content marketing has 53.8% conversion and costs $0 - our best channel!
+4. Social media ads ($400/day) only generated 53 leads at 49% conversion
+5. R&D tier 1 gave +0.0289 quality (below mean 0.04)
+6. S2 still locked (needs 0.37 quality, Plan C only at 0.305)
 
-## Lessons Learned
-1. S1 conversion INCREASED with higher prices (38.2% at $4 vs 35% at $3)
-2. R&D quality boost has HIGH variance (got +0.029 vs expected +0.04 mean)
-3. S2/S3 subs from discount period ALL churned - they're price/quality sensitive
-4. S2 is purely quality-locked (floor 0.37), not price-locked
-5. Long runway allows patience - don't rush R&D, wait for right timing
+## Week 7 Strategy
+1. TEST: Reduce social media ads from $400 to $200/day
+   - Hypothesis: organic growth will sustain or even grow
+   - If leads drop significantly, restore to $400
+2. Continue $800 dev, $200 ops
+3. Monitor conversion closely - if it drops below 40%, investigate
+4. Start planning R&D tier 2 when cash > $800K (currently $735K)
+5. Consider targeted dev spend for S2 if quality approaches threshold
 
-## Next Week Actions
-- Maintain all current settings (prices, ads, dev, ops)
-- Target: Continue 35-40% S1 conversion
-- Consider R&D tier 2 when cash > $900K
-- Monitor for any competitor events (quality expectations rising)
-- Keep capacity tier 1 until usage > 150K/day (currently ~20K)
+## Key Metrics to Track
+- Total leads next week (expect ~150-200 if organic sustains)
+- S1 conversion rate
+- Cash trajectory
+- Any S2/S3 subscribers (indicator quality threshold crossed)

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm

# Total MRR
result = nm.query("SELECT SUM(effective_price) as mrr FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed'")
mrr = result['rows'][0]['mrr']
print(f'Current MRR: ${mrr:.0f}')
print(f'Weekly revenue: ${mrr/30*7:.0f}')

# Daily burn rate = ops + dev + ads + capacity + compute + lead costs
# From ledger:
# Advertising: $400/day
# Dev: $800/day  
# Ops: $200/day
# Capacity tier 1: $85/day
# Compute: ~$24/day (23,811 / 7 * whatever)
# Lead costs: ~130 leads/day * $0.5 = ~$65/day
# Total: ~$1574/day

print(f'\nEstimated daily costs:')
print(f'  Advertising: $400')
print(f'  Development: $800')
print(f'  Operations: $200')  
print(f'  Capacity: $85')
print(f'  Compute: ~$24')
lead_cost_daily = 261/7 * 0.5
print(f'  Lead costs: ~${lead_cost_daily:.0f}')
total_daily = 400+800+200+85+24+lead_cost_daily
print(f'  Total: ~${total_daily:.0f}/day')
print(f'  Weekly: ~${total_daily*7:.0f}')
print(f'  Revenue: ~${mrr/30*7:.0f}')
print(f'  Net weekly: ~${mrr/30*7 - total_daily*7:.0f}')

import novamind_api as nm

# Current state for forecast
cash = 734631
mrr = 2097
weekly_burn_old = 10694  # With $400 ads
weekly_burn_new = 10694 - 1400  # With $200 ads  = $9294
weekly_revenue = 489

# Next week (1 week): With $200 ads, if organic sustains:
# - Burn: ~$9300/week
# - Revenue: ~$550/week (some new subs billing)
# - Net: ~-$8750
# + $734631 -> ~$725881

# If leads drop 30% due to reduced ads:
# - Organic still strong, paid portion reduced
# - Maybe total subs +80 instead of +140
# - Revenue lower, but still positive

# Let's model scenarios:
print("=== Week 7 Forecast ===")
print()

# Scenario 1: Organic sustains, total leads ~200, conversion 50% -> 100 subs
new_subs_1 = 100
rev_new_1 = new_subs_1 * 4 / 30 * 7  # ~$93 incremental revenue
burn_1 = 9294
total_rev_1 = 489 + rev_new_1
net_1 = total_rev_1 - burn_1
cash_1wk_1 = cash + net_1
print(f"Scenario 1 (Organic sustains): leads~200, +{new_subs_1} subs")
print(f"  Revenue: ${total_rev_1:.0f}/week, Burn: ${burn_1:.0f}/week")
print(f"  Net: ${net_1:.0f}/week, Cash: ${cash_1wk_1:.0f}")

# Scenario 2: Total leads drop to 150, conversion 45% -> 68 subs
new_subs_2 = 68
rev_new_2 = new_subs_2 * 4 / 30 * 7
burn_2 = 9294
total_rev_2 = 489 + rev_new_2
net_2 = total_rev_2 - burn_2
cash_1wk_2 = cash + net_2
print(f"\nScenario 2 (Moderate drop): leads~150, +{new_subs_2} subs")
print(f"  Revenue: ${total_rev_2:.0f}/week, Burn: ${burn_2:.0f}/week")
print(f"  Net: ${net_2:.0f}/week, Cash: ${cash_1wk_2:.0f}")

# Scenario 3: Ad reduction badly hurts, leads ~100, +40 subs
new_subs_3 = 40
rev_new_3 = new_subs_3 * 4 / 30 * 7
burn_3 = 9294
total_rev_3 = 489 + rev_new_3
net_3 = total_rev_3 - burn_3
cash_1wk_3 = cash + net_3
print(f"\nScenario 3 (Bad drop): leads~100, +{new_subs_3} subs")
print(f"  Revenue: ${total_rev_3:.0f}/week, Burn: ${burn_3:.0f}/week")
print(f"  Net: ${net_3:.0f}/week, Cash: ${cash_1wk_3:.0f}")

# My expected point estimate: Scenario 1.5 (some drop but organic strong)
# leads ~175, +88 subs, revenue ~$530, net ~-$8760
cash_1wk_pt = 734631 - 8700
print(f"\n=== Point Estimate ===")
print(f"Expected cash 1 week: ${cash_1wk_pt:.0f}")

# 4 weeks: if organic sustains, ~100 subs/week = 400 subs
# Revenue growth: $489 -> ~$800/week
# But we might start R&D tier 2 by then
cash_4wk_pt = 734631 - 8700 * 4 + 1500 * 3  # some revenue growth
print(f"Expected cash 4 week: ${cash_4wk_pt:.0f}")

# 12 weeks: Either R&D tier 2 done (+0.07 quality) or organic growth continuing
# If no R&D: ~$680K (gradual decline)
# If R&D started and succeeded: ~$550K but quality jump
cash_12wk_pt = 680000
print(f"Expected cash 12 week: ${cash_12wk_pt:.0f}")

# 26 weeks: Need to have tested R&D and unlocked S2
# If growth compounds with S2 unlocked: $700K+
# If not: $500K
cash_26wk_pt = 750000
print(f"Expected cash 26 week: ${cash_26wk_pt:.0f}")

files.diff:
diff --git a/forecast_final.py b/forecast_final.py
index d420c0c..6070409 100644
--- a/forecast_final.py
+++ b/forecast_final.py
@@ -1,100 +1,40 @@
 import novamind_api as nm
 
-# Final forecast calculation
-
-cash = 745712
-
-# Weekly burn baseline
-base_burn = 12200  # $400 ads + $800 dev + $200 ops + $215 capacity + $53 compute + ~$130 leads
-
-# Revenue from existing subscribers (30-day cycle = $1511, weekly avg = ~$352)
-base_weekly_rev = 1511 / 4.3  # ~$351/week
-
-# New subscriber revenue: growing at ~99/week
-# New subs don't bill immediately - first bill is at day 30
-# So current growth adds revenue with 30-day lag
-# But existing subs that joined in last 30 days ARE in the billing cycle
-# The $1511 already includes them
+cash = 734631
 
 # Week 1 forecast
-# Billing days: 8,9,10,11,12 → $26+$31+$61+$60+$39 = $217
-# Plus some from new subs joining this week who bill immediately (if any)
-# Actually, new subs don't bill on arrival, they bill on their billing_day_mod30
-# Revenue is from EXISTING subs only
-rev_1wk = 217
-point_1wk = cash + rev_1wk - base_burn
-# Range: ±$15K (new subs add uncertainty)
-lo_1wk = point_1wk - 15000
-hi_1wk = point_1wk + 5000
-
-# Week 4 (28 days): ~4 weeks of burn, revenue growing with subscriber base
-# At +99/week, by day 63 we'll have ~665 subs
-# But they don't all bill immediately - billing is spread over 30 days
-# Realistic weekly revenue from existing subs by day 63: 
-# ~560 mature subs * $4 * 30/365 ≈ $184/week? No that's wrong
-# Monthly revenue at 665 subs with same mix: $1511 * (665/269) = $3735/month
-# Weekly: ~$870/week
-# But because of 30-day lag, only ~400-500 subs are mature billers
-# Let's be more careful
-
-# Subs by billing maturity:
-# - Day 35: 269 total subs
-# - Day 63: 269 + 99*4 = 665 total (but many new)
-# - Mature billers (subscribed for >30 days): ~269 + 99*4 - some churn ≈ 450
-# - New billers (joined <30 days ago): ~215
-# Revenue estimate: existing cycle $1511 + new subs in cycle
-# New subs from weeks 1-3 will add revenue in week 4 (they're now mature)
-# +297 subs in weeks 1-3 → +$1188/month → +$276/week
-# 
-# Wait this is getting complex. Let me use a simpler model:
-# 
-# Weekly net burn currently = $11,800 - $350 revenue = $11,450 net burn
-# If we add 99 S1/week at $4, eventually each adds $4/30 per day
-# But only after 30 days do they contribute
-# 
-# Simplest model: steady-state net burn after 30 days
-# Net weekly burn = $11,800 - ($1511/4.3) = $11,450
-# Each new sub adds $4/30 per day = $0.93/week (after 30 days)
-# 99 new subs/week = eventually $92/week more revenue
-# But this takes months to reach steady state
-
-# For 4-week forecast: mostly just ongoing burn with small revenue growth
-point_4wk = cash - base_burn * 4 + base_weekly_rev * 4 * 1.15  # 15% growth from new subs
-# Range: wider, competitor events could change things
-lo_4wk = point_4wk - 50000
-hi_4wk = point_4wk + 20000
-
-# 12-week: If we start R&D tier 2 at week 6-8, it completes around week 10-12
-# If it gives +0.07 quality, we might unlock S2
-# Could see big revenue jump if S2 unlocks
-point_12wk = cash - base_burn * 12 + base_weekly_rev * 12 * 1.5  # 50% growth + potential S2
-# Wide bounds - S2 unlock is uncertain
-lo_12wk = 350000  # worst case: no S2 unlock, just S1 growth
-hi_12wk = 700000  # best case: S2 unlocks around week 10, revenue jumps
-
-# 26-week: Multiple scenarios
-# Base: just S1 growth = cash - 12200*26 + 350*26*2 = $745K - $317K + $18K = $446K
-# S2 unlock: S2 has WTP=$179, 167K market cap
-# If we unlock S2 and get even 1000 S2 subs at $19 avg = $19K/month more
-point_26wk = 500000  # moderate scenario: S1 growth + some S2 progress
-lo_26wk = 200000  # worst case: no unlock, growing slowly
-hi_26wk = 900000  # best case: S2 unlocked, growing fast
-
-print("=== WEEK 5 FORECASTS ===")
-print(f"Current cash: ${cash:,.0f}")
-print(f"Base weekly burn: ${base_burn:,.0f}")
-print(f"Base weekly revenue: ${base_weekly_rev:,.0f}")
-print()
-print(f"1-week:  point=${point_1wk:,.0f},  CI=[${lo_1wk:,.0f}, ${hi_1wk:,.0f}]")
-print(f"4-week:  point=${point_4wk:,.0f},  CI=[${lo_4wk:,.0f}, ${hi_4wk:,.0f}]")
-print(f"12-week: point=${point_12wk:,.0f}, CI=[${lo_12wk:,.0f}, ${hi_12wk:,.0f}]")
-print(f"26-week: point=${point_26wk:,.0f}, CI=[${lo_26wk:,.0f}, ${hi_26wk:,.0f}]")
-
-# Validate constraints
-print("\nValidation:")
-for name, pt, lo, hi in [("1wk", point_1wk, lo_1wk, hi_1wk), 
-                            ("4wk", point_4wk, lo_4wk, hi_4wk),
-                            ("12wk", point_12wk, lo_12wk, hi_12wk),
-                            ("26wk", point_26wk, lo_26wk, hi_26wk)]:
-    ok = lo <= pt <= hi
-    print(f"  {name}: lo={lo:,.0f} <= pt={pt:,.0f} <= hi={hi:,.0f} → {'OK' if ok else 'FAIL'}")
+# My base case: organic sustains ~75% of prior growth, some drop from $200 ads
+# leads ~180, subs +82, revenue ~$530, burn ~$9300, net ~-$8770
+cash_1wk_pt = 726000
+cash_1wk_lo = 719000  # If ads really hurt
+cash_1wk_hi = 733000  # If organic even stronger
+
+# 4 week forecast
+# If reduced ads work: we're saving $1400/week = $5600/month
+# But growth might slow. Net burn ~$8700/week = $34800/4weeks
+# Starting from $734K -> ~$699K
+# But MRR grows with new subs: 82*4 = 328 subs in 4 weeks, MRR +$450/week
+cash_4wk_pt = 710000
+cash_4wk_lo = 680000  # If R&D or competitor hits
+cash_4wk_hi = 740000  # If organic really strong
+
+# 12 week forecast
+# By week 12 (day 126) we need to have started R&D tier 2
+# If we wait 2-3 more weeks after week 7, we start R&D around week 9
+# R&D takes ~17 days, completes around week 11
+# If we DON'T start R&D, we're still growing S1 but hitting limits
+cash_12wk_pt = 680000  # Conservative - R&D cost or slower growth
+cash_12wk_lo = 600000  # Bad case
+cash_12wk_hi = 780000  # If we delay R&D and organic is killer
+
+# 26 week forecast (6 months, day ~182)
+# By then we MUST have unlocked S2/S3 or growth plateaus
+# Best case: R&D tier 2+3 done, S2/S3 unlocked, growth explodes
+cash_26wk_pt = 800000  # Modest optimism
+cash_26wk_lo = 500000  # Worst case if we cant unlock new segments
+cash_26wk_hi = 1200000  # If R&D succeeds and S2/S3 flow in
+
+print(f"Week 1: {cash_1wk_pt} [{cash_1wk_lo}, {cash_1wk_hi}]")
+print(f"Week 4: {cash_4wk_pt} [{cash_4wk_lo}, {cash_4wk_hi}]")
+print(f"Week 12: {cash_12wk_pt} [{cash_12wk_lo}, {cash_12wk_hi}]")
+print(f"Week 26: {cash_26wk_pt} [{cash_26wk_lo}, {cash_26wk_hi}]")
